component.name: Up
component.summary: Update and upgrade your Linux system

description.p: Up is a modern system update and upgrade application for Linux. It detects your OS package manager, Flatpak, Homebrew, and Nix, and updates them all with a single click. It also supports upgrading your distribution to the next major version with guided prerequisite checks and one-click execution.

Show a package-count popover instead of an inline expanding list for updated packages, so viewing them no longer grows the page or triggers the page scrollbar, and fix the updated-items list to reflect what was actually updated.

Add a VexOS cache-block dialog with just deploy/just update-all bypass options, a new cyan/orange brand theme, and layout improvements including a hero-row button layout and a terminal panel expanded by default.
Fix NixOS/VexOS false positive update detection, Flatpak false negative update detection, and move the update button to a fixed footer with a new Cancel action.
Fix Flatpak update check always reporting no updates available when Flatpak apps are installed system-wide.
Fix Nix update detection reporting "Everything is up to date" when the check fails silently, and fix stale Nix evaluation cache causing missed updates.

Fixed Nix build failing during installPhase because up-daemon was never compiled. Added cargoBuildFlags = ["--workspace"] so the full Cargo workspace is built, and corrected the postInstall path from the broken target/release/up-daemon to mv $out/bin/up-daemon $out/libexec/up-daemon.

Major architectural release. Introduces a privileged D-Bus backend service (up-daemon) with scoped polkit actions, proper cancellation, and audit logging — replacing the pkexec persistent shell. Adds a YAML plugin discovery system so community backends (apk, xbps, eopkg, and others) can be loaded from XDG data directories without modifying core code.

Major feature release adding fwupd firmware updates, pre-update snapshots (Snapper/Timeshift/btrfs), update changelog viewer, cancel-in-progress support, disk-space pre-check, scheduled background checks via systemd timer, GNU gettext localization infrastructure, and numerous reliability and security improvements across all backends.

Fixed Determinate Nix upgrades failing on non-NixOS distributions by running the upgrade unprivileged, since determinate-nixd communicates with its privileged daemon via IPC and does not require root on the client side.
Fixed Determinate Nix upgrades failing with a root privileges error by routing determinate-nixd upgrade through pkexec.
Fixed Nix and Flatpak running simultaneously. Restored real-time Nix terminal output by forcing line-buffered output via stdbuf and adding --print-build-logs to nixos-rebuild switch.
First stable release. Supports APT, DNF, Pacman, Zypper, Flatpak, Homebrew, and Nix backends with live streaming output, distro version upgrades, and a native libadwaita interface.
